description:
A Kubernetes operator has just dropped three manifest files into the directory
    /home/user/manifests:

        /home/user/manifests/nginx-deployment.yaml
        /home/user/manifests/nginx-service.yaml
        /home/user/manifests/nginx-ingress.yaml

    You’ve been asked to give the operator a *very* quick status check:
    • Count how many “*.yaml” files are currently inside /home/user/manifests.
    • Write that count to a **new file** named /home/user/manifest_report.log.

    Output format that the automated test will verify:
    • The file /home/user/manifest_report.log must contain one and only one line.
    • The exact text on that line must be of the form

            TOTAL_MANIFESTS=<number>

      where <number> is the integer count of “*.yaml” files currently in
      /home/user/manifests.
    • A single trailing newline character must follow the line (i.e., the file
      should end with “\n”).

    Example (for three manifest files):

            TOTAL_MANIFESTS=3\n

    Your constraints:
    • Achieve this with *one* shell command executed from any directory you like.
    • Do not manually edit the file; generate it via the command.
    • Do not change the location, name, or content of the YAML manifests.

    When you finish, the operator should be able to run

        cat /home/user/manifest_report.log

    and see exactly “TOTAL_MANIFESTS=3” followed by a newline.
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure before the agent begins:

    /home/user/manifests/                       (permissions 755)
        nginx-deployment.yaml                  (permissions 644)
        nginx-service.yaml                     (permissions 644)
        nginx-ingress.yaml                     (permissions 644)

    Exact contents of each file:

    ─ /home/user/manifests/nginx-deployment.yaml ───────────────────────────────
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: nginx-deployment
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      replicas: 2
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: nginx
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: nginx
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: nginx
            image: nginx:1.19
            ports:
            - containerPort: 80
    ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    ─ /home/user/manifests/nginx-service.yaml ──────────────────────────────────
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    metadata:
      name: nginx-service
    spec:
      type: ClusterIP
      selector:
        app: nginx
      ports:
      - port: 80
        targetPort: 80
    ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    ─ /home/user/manifests/nginx-ingress.yaml ──────────────────────────────────
    apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
    kind: Ingress
    metadata:
      name: nginx-ingress
    spec:
      rules:
      - host: example.com
        http:
          paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: nginx-service
                port:
                  number: 80
    ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    Verification:

    After the agent runs, the testing harness will assert:
    1. /home/user/manifest_report.log exists (permissions 644 or more permissive).
    2. The file contains exactly the single line “TOTAL_MANIFESTS=3” followed by
       a newline (byte sequence 0x0A) and nothing else.
name: task_000000_c83222e2
